rulings.Case Title: CANDICE CLARK, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS SUCCESSOR IN et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.'S Motion To Compel Answers To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories And Requests For Production, To Compel Further Answers, And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS DISCOVERY CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, JANUARY 14, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 1, Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.'s motion to compel plaintiffs to serve answers to defendant's form interrogatories, special interrogatories and requests for production, and to compel further answers is granted. Defendant's request for sanctions of $2000 is granted. No opposition filed. Plaintiffs have 10 days to serve responses and verifications. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m., not 9:00a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JUDITH SHRUM et al VS. 3M COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories (Set One) And Requests For Production Of Documents (Set One), To Deem Admitted Its Requests For Admission(Set One), And For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiffs And Their Counsel
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS DISCOVERY CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, JANUARY 14, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 2, Defendant Crown Cork & Seal Company (USA), Inc.'s motion to compel responses to special interrogatories and requests for production, to deem admitted its requests for admission and for sanctions is off calendar at the moving party's request. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: HAROLD KOEPKE et al VS. FORD MOTOR COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs? Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Defendant Berry Berry?S Amended Notice Of Taking Deposition Of Harold Koepke And Request For Production Of Documents, Photographs And Things Or, In The Alternative, Motion For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS DISCOVERY CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, JANUARY 14, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 3, Plaintiffs' motion to quash Berry & Berry's amended notice of taking deposition of Harold Koepke or, in the alternative, motion for protective order: hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:45a.m., not 9:00a.m. Both sides must bring proposed orders to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)
